definition_population.content: Immunodeficiency 37 is caused by biallelic loss-of-function BCL10 mutations, disrupting the CARD11-BCL10-MALT1 (CBM) signalosome downstream of antigen receptors; can present as SCID escaping newborn screening.

sources.quote: BCL10 Deficiency Presenting as Severe Combined Immunodeficiency Escaping Newborn Screening.

epidemiology.content: No population-level prevalence/incidence figure found; documented via individual case reports; grouped with CARD11/MALT1 deficiencies as 'germline CBM-opathies' with heterogeneous clinical phenotypes.

sources.quote: Germline mutations that alter the function of members of this complex (termed CBM-opathies) cause a broad array of clinical phenotypes, ranging

biology.content: BCL10 forms heterotrimers with MALT1 and CARD-family adaptors essential for NF-kB activation in innate and adaptive immunity; inherited BCL10 deficiency impairs both hematopoietic and nonhematopoietic immunity; CBM complex operates directly downstream of the TCR.

sources.quote: Heterotrimers composed of B cell CLL/lymphoma 10 (BCL10), mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue lymphoma translocation protein 1 (MALT1), and caspase recruitment domain-containing (CARD) family adaptors play a role in NF-kB activation
Inherited BCL10 deficiency impairs hematopoietic and nonhematopoietic immunity

treatment.content: No FDA-approved disease-specific pharmacologic therapy identified in this pass; general combined-immunodeficiency management (supportive care, HSCT) applies by analogy, but no BCL10-specific FDA drug application found.

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov search for 'BCL10 deficiency combined immunodeficiency' returned 0 total trial records.

sources.quote: total=0 (search_trials condition=BCL10 deficiency combined immunodeficiency, count_total=True)

gaps: No clinical trials identified specific to this indication
